title: Add Capsule to safely run untrusted code using WebAssembly sandboxes
body_text:
### Checked other resources

- [x] This is a feature request, not a bug report or usage question.
- [x] I added a clear and descriptive title that summarizes the feature request.
- [x] I used the GitHub search to find a similar feature request and didn't find it.
- [x] I checked the LangChain documentation and API reference to see if this feature already exists.
- [x] This is not related to the langchain-community package.

### Package (Required)

- [x] langchain
- [ ] langchain-openai
- [ ] langchain-anthropic
- [ ] langchain-classic
- [ ] langchain-core
- [ ] langchain-model-profiles
- [ ] langchain-tests
- [ ] langchain-text-splitters
- [ ] langchain-chroma
- [ ] langchain-deepseek
- [ ] langchain-exa
- [ ] langchain-fireworks
- [ ] langchain-groq
- [ ] langchain-huggingface
- [ ] langchain-mistralai
- [ ] langchain-nomic
- [ ] langchain-ollama
- [ ] langchain-openrouter
- [ ] langchain-perplexity
- [ ] langchain-qdrant
- [ ] langchain-xai
- [ ] Other / not sure / general

### Feature Description

I would like LangChain to support Capsule as a lightweight, self-hosted sandbox for executing untrusted Python/JavaScript code. Capsule is a runtime that runs python/javascript code in WebAssembly sandboxes.

### Use Case

When AI agents generate code, executing it directly could be risky for the host system. Except for Docker containers or cloud-based tools, there are few lightweight alternatives. While `langchain-sandbox` (Pyodide/Wasm) seems to be a great solution, it executes Python code only and is now archived.

### Proposed Solution

Integrate `langchain-capsule` to documentation, a LangChain version of Capsule. Here's how the implementation works:
```python
import asyncio
from langchain_capsule import CapsulePythonTool, CapsuleJSTool

# Python Example
python_tool = CapsulePythonTool()
result = python_tool.run("1 + 1")
print(result) # "2"

# JavaScript / TypeScript Example
js_tool = CapsuleJSTool()
result = asyncio.run(js_tool.arun("1 + 2"))
print(result) # "3"
```

Only the first run takes a second (cold start), then every next run starts in ~10ms. This is because Capsule compiles and caches a native version of the Wasm module locally after the first execution.

### Alternatives Considered

_No response_

### Additional Context

- PyPI package: langchain-capsule
- Integration repo: [github.com/mavdol/langchain-capsule](https://github.com/mavdol/langchain-capsule)
- Capsule main repo: [github.com/mavdol/capsule](https://github.com/mavdol/capsule)

Happy to submit a PR in the LangChain docs if there's interest. Also open to other integration possibilities around Capsule!

entities.name: LangChain
entities.description: A framework for developing applications powered by language models, for which the feature is requested.

entities.name: Capsule
entities.description: A runtime that safely executes untrusted Python/JavaScript code within WebAssembly sandboxes.

entities.name: WebAssembly Sandboxes
entities.description: A secure, isolated environment for running code, utilized by Capsule and `langchain-sandbox`.

entities.name: Untrusted Code Execution
entities.description: The security risk posed by running code generated by AI agents directly on a host system.

entities.name: AI Agents
entities.description: Systems capable of generating code, which can lead to security risks if their output is executed without sandboxing.

entities.name: Docker Containers
entities.description: A virtualization technology used for isolating applications, mentioned as an alternative to Capsule for sandboxing.

entities.name: Cloud-based Tools
entities.description: External services offering sandboxed execution environments, mentioned as an alternative to Capsule.

entities.name: LangChain Sandbox
entities.description: An existing, now archived, LangChain component that provided Python code execution using Pyodide/Wasm.

entities.name: Pyodide
entities.description: A Python distribution compiled to WebAssembly, used by `langchain-sandbox`.

entities.name: LangChain Capsule
entities.description: The proposed integration package for Capsule within the LangChain ecosystem.

entities.name: CapsulePythonTool
entities.description: A tool within `langchain-capsule` designed for executing Python code.

entities.name: CapsuleJSTool
entities.description: A tool within `langchain-capsule` designed for executing JavaScript/TypeScript code.

entities.name: Cold Start
entities.description: The initial delay experienced when a Capsule module is run for the first time.

entities.name: Python Code Only Execution
entities.description: A limitation of `langchain-sandbox` where it could only execute Python code.

entities.name: Archived Status
entities.description: The state of `langchain-sandbox` indicating it is no longer actively maintained.

extracted_data.summary: This feature request proposes integrating `langchain-capsule` to enable safe, lightweight execution of untrusted Python/JavaScript code generated by AI agents using WebAssembly sandboxes, addressing limitations of existing solutions like `langchain-sandbox`.
